title: gilia tenella
intro: Gilia tenella, also known as sticky gilia, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It possesses medicinal properties that have been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and inflammation. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in herbal preparations to promote relaxation, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems, and are also known for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Gilia tenella has been used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions for its health benefits, which include reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function.
